biologics_rheumatology.policy_reference: Standard Clinical Policy Bulletin — Biologic DMARDs

criteria.why_required: Biologics are high-cost; insurer requires specialist-confirmed diagnosis
criteria.sufficient_evidence: Clinical note from rheumatologist (or relevant specialist) confirming diagnosis with ICD-10 code within 12 months
criteria.common_failure: GP letter used instead of specialist note

criteria.criterion: Trial and failure of first-line conventional DMARD therapy
criteria.why_required: Step therapy protocol — biologics only after conventional DMARDs fail
criteria.sufficient_evidence: Prescription records showing DMARD (methotrexate, hydroxychloroquine, sulfasalazine) prescribed for minimum 90 days + clinical note documenting inadequate response or adverse effect
criteria.common_failure: Trial duration not explicitly stated, or adverse effect not documented in clinical notes

criteria.why_required: Confirms active disease requiring biologic therapy
criteria.sufficient_evidence: DAS28, CDAI, or SDAI score documented within last 6 months showing moderate-to-severe disease
criteria.common_failure: Score present but dated more than 6 months ago

criteria.criterion: No active serious infection, TB, or hepatitis B
criteria.why_required: Biologics contraindicated with active infections — patient safety
criteria.sufficient_evidence: TB screening (IGRA or TST) result within 12 months — negative or treated latent TB. Hepatitis B serology on file.
criteria.common_failure: TB test never ordered or result not in chart

criteria.why_required: Insurer requires specialist initiation for biologic therapy
criteria.sufficient_evidence: Prescriber NPI number matches rheumatology/dermatology/gastroenterology specialty on file
criteria.common_failure: PCP ordering biologic without specialist co-signature

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Physician note documenting specific symptoms, duration, and clinical question the MRI will answer
criteria.common_failure: Vague indication like 'rule out pathology' without specific symptom documentation

criteria.criterion: Conservative treatment or lower-cost imaging tried first
criteria.sufficient_evidence: Documentation that X-ray, CT, or conservative treatment was tried and was insufficient
criteria.common_failure: No prior imaging documented even when clinically appropriate

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Physician statement explaining how MRI results will alter treatment plan
criteria.common_failure: Not explicitly stated in the referral

uk_skilled_worker_visa.request_type: UK Skilled Worker Visa (formerly Tier 2 General)
uk_skilled_worker_visa.policy_reference: Home Office Immigration Rules Appendix Skilled Worker

criteria.sufficient_evidence: CoS reference number from a licensed UK sponsor employer. Must be issued within 3 months of application.
criteria.common_failure: CoS expired or sponsor licence revoked after CoS issued

criteria.criterion: Job meets eligible occupation skill level (RQF Level 3+)
criteria.sufficient_evidence: Job title and SOC code confirmed on the eligible occupations list. Job offer letter with role description.
criteria.common_failure: Job description doesn't match the SOC code used on CoS

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Salary on CoS meets the higher of: £26,200 per year, £10.75 per hour, or the 'going rate' for occupation. Offer letter confirming salary.
criteria.common_failure: Part-time salary annualised incorrectly, or going rate for occupation exceeds general threshold

criteria.sufficient_evidence: IELTS Academic/UKVI score B1 (4.0+) in all components, OR passport from majority English-speaking country, OR degree taught in English with Ecctis confirmation
criteria.common_failure: Certificate expired (must be within 2 years for IELTS UKVI), or wrong IELTS test type used

criteria.sufficient_evidence: £1,270 held in personal bank account for minimum 28 consecutive days ending no more than 31 days before application. Bank statements required.
criteria.common_failure: 28-day window not met, or funds dip below threshold during the period

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Current valid passport with expiry beyond intended stay. Scan of bio data page.
criteria.common_failure: Passport expires during intended visa period

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Certificate from UKVI-approved clinic for applicants from listed countries (includes most of Africa, Asia, South America). Test valid for 6 months.
criteria.common_failure: Test done at non-approved clinic, or result expired

criteria.sufficient_evidence: 2 years of filed accounts (Companies House), OR 2 years of tax returns (SA302 / CT600)
criteria.common_failure: Only 1 year filed, or accounts not yet filed for most recent year

criteria.sufficient_evidence: 6 consecutive months of business bank statements showing regular trading activity, salary payments, and positive cash flow
criteria.common_failure: Personal account used instead of business account, or months missing

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Written business plan including: executive summary, market analysis, use of funds, 3-year P&L projections, cash flow forecast
criteria.common_failure: Projections present but no explanation of assumptions, or projections unrealistically optimistic

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Description of assets offered as security OR signed personal guarantee from director(s) with evidence of personal assets
criteria.common_failure: No collateral identified and director unwilling to provide personal guarantee

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Companies House registration number, certificate of incorporation, registered address confirmation
criteria.common_failure: Sole trader without formal registration documentation

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Photo ID (passport or driving licence) + proof of address (utility bill or bank statement, within 3 months) for all directors holding 25%+ equity
criteria.common_failure: Address proof more than 3 months old

criteria.sufficient_evidence: List of all current business loans, credit facilities, leases, and outstanding HMRC liabilities with current balances
criteria.common_failure: HMRC arrears not disclosed, or outstanding bounce-back loan not mentioned

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Registered charity number or legal entity documentation. Evidence of operation in funder's geographic/sector focus area.
criteria.common_failure: Organisation type not explicitly stated as eligible in funder guidelines

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Explicit statement mapping project objectives to the funder's stated funding priorities (use funder's own language)
criteria.common_failure: Generic project description that doesn't reference funder's specific priorities

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Clear logic model: inputs → activities → outputs → outcomes → impact. Evidence from similar interventions.
criteria.common_failure: Activities described well but outcomes not quantified or evidenced

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Line-item budget with cost justification for each item. Salary costs with FTE percentages. Overhead rate explained.
criteria.common_failure: Lump sums without breakdown, or overhead higher than funder's cap

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Specific, measurable indicators. Data collection methodology. Reporting schedule.
criteria.common_failure: Vague statements like 'we will monitor progress' without specific indicators

criteria.criterion: Policy was active at time of incident
criteria.sufficient_evidence: Policy schedule showing coverage dates that include incident date. Premium payment confirmation.
criteria.common_failure: Policy lapsed due to missed payment before incident

criteria.criterion: Incident type is a covered peril
criteria.sufficient_evidence: Policy wording confirming the specific type of damage/loss is listed as a covered event
criteria.common_failure: Assuming all damage is covered — flood vs water damage, wear vs sudden damage distinctions

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Claim reported within policy-specified window (typically 30 days of incident). Written notification date on record.
criteria.common_failure: Delayed reporting due to uncertainty about coverage

criteria.criterion: Proof of loss and damage documentation
criteria.sufficient_evidence: Photographs of damage taken promptly. Written description of incident. Independent assessor report if applicable.
criteria.common_failure: No photos taken, or damaged items disposed of before assessment

criteria.sufficient_evidence: Original receipts, credit card statements, valuations, or manufacturer RRP for claimed items
criteria.common_failure: Estimated values without supporting evidence
